title: Hippeastrum brevipes
taxonomy: Hippeastrum brevipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to South America. It is commonly known as a type of amaryllis. The plant is classified as a type of bulbous perennial, characterized by its long, thin leaves and showy flowers. Within the genus Hippeastrum, H. brevipes is one of several species, each with unique characteristics and growth habits. As a member of the Amaryllidaceae family, H. brevipes is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as the popular amaryllis flowers.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain in the body.
